name: Nutella
description: The Nutella method is an advanced Pyraminx speedsolving technique known for its low move count and creative solving approach. It begins by solving the top layer with a specific 3-color front face constraint, then simultaneously solves centers and the top, and finishes with L3E. The method's strength lies in its efficiency and the simultaneous nature of its middle step.

steps.name: Top with 3-Color Front
steps.description: Solve the top layer while ensuring the front face displays exactly three colors. This constraint sets up the puzzle for an efficient simultaneous solve in the next step.
tips: The 3-color front constraint is key — do not skip it
Practice identifying the best top/front orientation quickly

steps.name: Simultaneous Center + Top Finish
steps.description: Solve the centers and complete the top layer simultaneously. This step exploits the 3-color front constraint to solve multiple elements at once, keeping the move count very low.
tips: This step flows naturally from a correct first step
Learn the small set of cases that arise from the 3-color setup
Speed comes from recognizing the simultaneous solve patterns

steps.name: Last 3 Edges (L3E)
steps.description: Solve the final three edges using L3E algorithms. The setup from previous steps often results in favorable L3E cases.
tips: Anticipate the L3E case during the previous step
Favorable cases occur more often with good setup
Full L3E knowledge allows one-looking this step

pros: Very low move count (9-13 moves average)
Simultaneous solving step is highly efficient
Competitive at the highest level of Pyraminx solving
Elegant and creative approach to the puzzle
cons: Requires strong understanding of Pyraminx mechanics
3-color front constraint takes time to master
Fewer learning resources compared to LBL or top-first methods
Recognition for the simultaneous step is difficult to develop
